rules.title: Azure Storage shared-key connection string
rules.fix: Replace with short-lived SAS issued by an authenticated backend; rotate the existing account key.

rules.title: DB connection string with embedded password
PuTTY private key (.ppk) material
rules.fix: Remove embedded SSH private keys; provision per-device keys at runtime from a secure store.

rules.title: RSA/DSA private key in XML (RSAKeyValue with D)

deser_tokens.title: Newtonsoft.Json TypeNameHandling reference
deser_tokens.description: Auto/Objects/All on attacker-reachable input enables RCE via $type gadget chains.

deser_tokens.title: BinaryFormatter reference
deser_tokens.description: BinaryFormatter is unsafe and obsolete (CWE-502). Confirm runtimeconfig EnableUnsafeBinaryFormatterSerialization=false.

callsite_patterns.title: Custom certificate-validation callback present, verify it doesn't return true unconditionally
callsite_patterns.description: Static reference only. Decompile the method to confirm a real check happens.

callsite_patterns.title: Reference to weak hash (MD5/SHA1)
callsite_patterns.description: Acceptable for non-security uses; never for integrity, signatures, or password hashing.

callsite_patterns.title: System.Random reference (non-cryptographic RNG)
callsite_patterns.description: Use RandomNumberGenerator/RNGCryptoServiceProvider for tokens, IVs, salts, keys.

callsite_patterns.title: Raw ADO.NET command construction (verify SQL is parameterized)
callsite_patterns.description: Presence of a raw command object is not itself a bug. Decompile the call site: if the CommandText is built by string concatenation/interpolation with external input, this is SQL injection. Parameterized queries are safe.

callsite_patterns.title: Process/shell execution API (verify arguments are not attacker-controlled)
callsite_patterns.description: Decompile the call site. If the filename or arguments incorporate external input (config, IPC, URL, file content) without validation, this is command/argument injection. UseShellExecute=true with a user-controlled verb/path is especially dangerous.

callsite_patterns.title: Clipboard access API
callsite_patterns.description: App reads or writes the system clipboard. Reading can sniff credentials/secrets a user copied from another app; writing can plant content. Confirm the clipboard is not logged or transmitted.

callsite_patterns.title: Global input capture / screen capture API (keylogger/screenshot surface)
callsite_patterns.description: Low-level keyboard/mouse hooks, async key state, or full-screen BitBlt/CopyFromScreen indicate global input or screen capture. Legitimate for some tools, but confirm the captured data is not exfiltrated or logged in cleartext.

callsite_patterns.title: Token impersonation API (verify RevertToSelf is always called)
callsite_patterns.description: Impersonation primitives. If the app impersonates a client (e.g. over a named pipe) and fails to RevertToSelf on every path -- or impersonates without validating the caller -- a low-priv client can drive a privileged action (EoP).

callsite_patterns.title: Environment-variable lookup (verify not used for DLL/exe/config paths)
callsite_patterns.description: If an environment variable a standard user controls is used to build a DLL/EXE/config path that the app then loads or executes, a user can redirect it (DLL planting / path hijack). Decompile to see what the value feeds.

callsite_patterns.title: LDAP / directory query API (verify filter is escaped)
callsite_patterns.description: If the LDAP filter or DN is built from external input without escaping, this is LDAP injection. Confirm the filter uses parameterized/escaped values.

callsite_patterns.title: Path building / archive extraction (verify traversal is blocked)
callsite_patterns.description: Path.Combine with external input, or zip extraction without entry-name validation, allows ../ traversal (zip-slip) writing outside the intended directory. Confirm extracted/combined paths are canonicalized and constrained.

callsite_patterns.title: Broken/weak symmetric cipher (DES / 3DES / RC2 / RC4)
callsite_patterns.description: DES (56-bit), 3DES (sweet32), RC2 and RC4 are broken or deprecated. If used to protect anything sensitive, the protection is ineffective. Confirm the cipher and key handling; replace with AES-GCM.
